preamble: The shift from the neutral schwa [ə] to the alveolar nasal [n] occurs through the process of syllabic nasal development. As a reduced vowel is increasingly shortened in speech, it can be entirely absorbed by a following nasal consonant, which then becomes the functional nucleus of the syllable.

examples.note: The schwa is lost, and the nasal consonant becomes the syllable nucleus.
Historical vowel-nasal sequences merged into nasalized vowels, showing the close articulatory relationship between reduced vowels and nasals.
